source.summary: Layered 429s observed during rolling API token swapback causing throughput collapse
source.description:
Issue summary: Customer (Acme Analytics, dedicated) reported sudden and sustained 429s beginning ~2026-03-10T14:20Z that coincided with a scheduled rolling API key rotation. Impact: interactive chat and batch embed pipelines degraded; sustained 30-60% request rejection for 20 minutes and client-side retry storms. Environment: prod-us-west (us-west-2), dedicated pool id dp-88. Customer reports that their orchestrator performs aggressive short retries and they observed token swaps in their logs.

Initial hypothesis: rolling credential refresh led to swapback behavior where some ingress proxies temporarily presented rotated keys while backend accounting still counted against the old token windows. This created token-window misalignment and effective fanout of short-lived keys across pooled connections, exhausting tenant burst buckets and triggering layered 429s.

Why ticket created: customer-facing outage with measurable impact and unclear root cause; require SRE/infra follow-up and mitigation guidance for the customer to restore stability and avoid recurrence.
steps_to_reproduce: 1) In a dedicated pool, schedule a rolling API key rotation across fronting proxies and orchestrators.
2) Simulate client orchestrator with 80 parallel workers each doing short-lived sessions and exponential retry with 200-500ms base backoff.
3) Observe that some requests hit edge instances with rotated keys while others still hit instances with previous refill state; watch tenant burst tokens and 429s spike.
4) Correlate with token-refill logs and refill timestamps in rate-limiter metrics to see drift during swapback window.
logs: 2026-03-10T14:21:03Z EDGE PROXY dp-88 connection 10.2.3.7 requestId=abc123 status=429 code=RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED tenant=acme-ts tokens_left=0 window_reset=2026-03-10T14:21:10Z
2026-03-10T14:21:04Z AUTH swap event: key=ak_live_xxx rotated -> ak_live_yyy origin=orchestrator-12
2026-03-10T14:21:05Z RATE-LIMITER refill mismatch detected: observed_refill_ts=2026-03-10T14:20:40Z expected_refill_ts=2026-03-10T14:21:00Z drift=-20s
2026-03-10T14:21:06Z ORCH worker-45 retry=3 backoff=300ms requestId=abc127 result=429
source.observation_summary: SRE trace and metrics show a tight window (~14:20:50Z - 14:22:30Z) where token accounting diverged across edge instances. Short-lived sessions retried aggressively and were multiplexed over a small set of pooled connections, causing double-counting against tenant burst buckets. Metrics: tenant_burst_tokens went to zero and refill_rate showed transient negative slope; connection_reuse_high and swap_event_count spiked during the window.
investigation_notes: Checked customer request traces and internal edge metrics; observed high correlation between swap events and 429 spikes.
Replayed small-scale rotation in staging and reproduced similar 429 waves when orchestrator retried aggressively and connections were pooled behind the same ephemeral IPs.

Observed rate-limiter logs indicating refill timestamp mismatch on ~7% of edge backends during the swap—likely race in auth handshake path where token window being applied to rotated key before refill index advanced.

Hypothesis refined: 'swapback' occurs when a rotated credential is presented briefly on one edge path while upstream accounting remains on previous key's window, causing duplicate token consumption across perceived keys and draining tenant burst budgets faster than expected.
Cross-referenced with INT-4201 which tracks a recent edge-proxy connection-affinity change that may increase connection reuse during rotation windows.
source.customer_impact_details: Acme reported client-side retries increased latency to 3-8s for interactive flows and ~25% of batch embeds failed without successful retry within client policy. Business impact: a spike in failed document ingestion jobs for a nightly sync and temporarily degraded user-facing recommendations. Customer has requested concrete mitigation and prevention guidance.
source.temporary_mitigation_provided:
1) Advised Acme to pause non-critical background workers for 30 minutes and stagger worker restart to reduce fanout.
2) Recommended enabling client-side exponential backoff with jitter and increasing base backoff to 500-800ms for the rotation window.
3) Suggested a one-off bump to their dedicated pool burst capacity (support-approved) while we stabilize (applied at 14:40Z).
4) Provided a short checklist for next rotations: stagger orchestration, avoid simultaneous connection resets, and avoid pooling too many short-lived sessions on few hosts.
comments: Maya Rodriguez (2026-03-10T14:35Z): Added initial customer log bundle and timeline from Acme. Customer says rotation was scheduled via their CI at 14:20Z.
Ethan Park (2026-03-10T14:50Z): Confirmed we see matching spike on edge-proxy metrics. Tentatively marking as Sev1 and pulling in rate-limiter owners.

SRE Lead - Anika Bose (2026-03-10T15:12Z): Running focused experiment in staging to reproduce. Found a race where auth middleware applied new key header before rate-limiter service finished refilling window. PR referenced: https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/8432
Support Engineer - Leo Chen (2026-03-10T17:05Z): Applied temporary burst bump for dp-88; customer reports reduction in 429s after 14:40Z. Monitoring for recurrence.

Customer - Acme SRE (Jordan Miller) (2026-03-11T09:10Z): Thanks — we applied stagger on our orchestrator retries per checklist and saw steady traffic for the night. Need permanent fix so rotations don't require manual steps.
source.root_cause: Race between credential rotation propagation and rate-limiter refill state leading to duplicate counting of token consumption across swapped keys on a subset of edge instances. Connection pooling and aggressive short retries amplified the effect into a tenant-level burst exhaustion.
source.resolution: WIP: Short-term mitigation applied (burst bump + customer retry guidance) and rotation checklist documented. Long-term fix: engineering to land a guard in auth middleware to delay new-key acceptance until edge rate-limiter acknowledges refill index advance (PR #8432). Also plan to add a metric for 'credential_swap_window' and a console warning when rotations are in-progress for dedicated pools.
next_steps: ENG-1: Land PR to add handshake gating between auth and rate-limiter (owner: @anika, target: 2026-03-18)
ENG-2: Add alert and dashboard for credential_swap_rate and swapback events (owner: observability team, target: 2026-03-20)
OPS-1: Create a short rotation-runbook with recommended stagger patterns and client retry guidance; share with customer-facing docs (owner: support, due: 2026-03-15)
CS-1: Follow up with Acme SRE to schedule next rotation test window and validate non-regression (owner: Maya, due: 2026-03-17)
source.postmortem_plan: If PR fixes land and metrics show no recurrence for two full scheduled rotations across dedicated pools, close incident and publish brief postmortem. If recurrence observed, escalate to incident review board and open follow-up epic for refactor of token accounting path.
source.public_response_snippet: We identified a transient race during credential rotations that could cause duplicated token consumption on a subset of edge hosts. We have applied a temporary capacity increase and provided guidance to affected customers; a code fix is in progress to ensure rotations are atomic with rate-limiter state updates.
